title: Tephroseris hieraciiformis
taxonomy: Tephroseris hieraciiformis is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Tephroseris, which is a group of flowering plants. Tephroseris hieraciiformis is further classified as a species of flowering plant, specifically within the subsection Hieracioides. The plant's classification is based on its morphology, phylogenetics, and other biological characteristics. Its classification has been studied and confirmed through various botanical research and identification methods.

benefits: Tephroseris hieraciiformis, commonly known as Hieracium, is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. intro: Tephroseris hieraciiformis, also known as the Hawk's Beard or Hieracium, is a flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris hieraciiformis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome and ulcers.
